title: My First Python Script for Network Monitoring
summary: A beginner lab on parsing router outputs for BGP, CPU, and interface issues.
content: I built a small Python script to read router outputs and print a health summary. This helped me understand split(), strip(), loops, and basic automation logic for network engineering use cases.

title: Understanding Python Projects in 3 Simple Layers
summary: A beginner-friendly explanation of how Python projects work using a house-building analogy: environment, package manager, and libraries.
image: images/Building Python programming step by step.png
content:
When I started learning Python for network automation, I realized that running a Python project is not just about writing code. There is a structure behind it.

🏗️ Think of a Python project like building your own house

🔹 Environment (venv) → Your land / workspace
It keeps your project isolated and avoids conflicts with other projects.
Commands:
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate

🔹 Package Manager (pip) → Your tool store
Installs and manages libraries. You go there to download what your project needs.
Command:
pip install --upgrade pip

🔹 Libraries (pyyaml, netmiko) → Your tools & materials
Each library solves a specific problem.
- pyyaml → Read YAML files
- netmiko → Network automation
Command:
pip install pyyaml

🧠 No land → nowhere to build
🧰 No tool store → nothing gets done
🧱 No tools/materials → no functionality

🚀 Master these 3 layers and you can build any Python project confidently, especially in network automation.
